item.title: Australian cyber experts warn of the 'catastrophic' threat quantum computing could pose
item.description: They have good reason to be nervous - the emergence of quantum computing looms as the biggest global security challenge in decades.

item.title: Experts bust common food myths, including one that half of all Aussies believe
item.description: It is one of many myths the Food Information Safety Council is looking to bust.

item.title: COVID-19 vaccines carry substantially less risk to kids' heart health than infection, new study finds
item.description: Children who catch COVID-19 are at risk of contracting dangerous heart inflammation, but don't face the same danger from getting vaccinated against the virus.

item.title: REVIEW: As a long-lapsed Pokemon fan, it feels like Legends Z-A was made for me. But this is my only wish for
item.description: REVIEW: Is the franchise headed in the right direction? Yes. Is it the perfect Pokemon game? No.

item.title: Experimental daily pill may lower cholesterol in patients who don't see enough benefit with statins alone
item.description: It is similar to other pills available in Australia already.

item.title: 'Never wash raw chicken': More than half of Australians risking higher chance of food poisoning with common ha
item.description: P﻿eople think this measure is protecting them, but experts warn it could do the opposite.

item.title: Brisbane and Gold Coast residents warned after measles cases detected at concert, tourist attractions
item.description: Residents of two major cities are being told to be on high alert.

item.title: Cost of treatment for deadly cancer slashed dramatically after PBS addition
item.description: Those with multiple myeloma, an incurable blood cancer, would previously pay up to $150,000 for treatment.

item.title: Amyl and the Sniffers' free gig shut down over security fears at Fed Square
item.description: The band paid for $35,000 worth of drink for disappointed fans.

item.title: Parents of teens tragically killed by methanol poisoning team up with government for schoolies abroad warning
item.description: The mothers of two Melbourne teenagers who tragically died last year have teamed up with the government in a warning campaign for young travellers.

item.title: New mRNA flu vaccine far more effective than existing jabs, but has some limitations, study finds
item.description: A newly developed mRNA-based flu vaccine is almost 35 per cent more effective against the most common form of influenza, according to a new study.

item.title: ‘The zombies have evolved’: Interview with Resident Evil Requiem producer
item.description: Capcom, the developers of the classic Resident Evil franchise, are gearing up for their ninth major entry in the long running series — Resident Evil Requiem. 9news.com.au caught up with the game’s producer.

item.title: 'Double-edged sword': How Aussie content creators are reacting to the under-16 social media ban
item.description: A﻿s the under-16 social media ban inches closer, questions are being raised about the implications the ban will have on people who target their content toward younger audiences.

item.title: Everything we know about ChatGPT's 'erotica' rollout in December
item.description: Experts from a range of fields, including OpenAI's own wellbeing panel, are worried about what it means for mental health.

item.title: Review: The best game on the Switch 2 yet
item.description: It meets (and arguably exceeds) the exceptionally high bar set by its predecessors and is willing to gamble the franchise's small but loyal fanbase on a few big changes.

item.title: How Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora's From the Ashes expansion aligns with the new film
item.description: Developed in collaboration between Massive Entertainment and Lightstorm Entertainment, the From the Ashes game expansion runs side by side to the events of Avatar: Fire and Ash.

item.title: Apple issues urgent iPhone update after triple zero emergency call testing raises concerns
item.description: Apple has released an emergency update to iOS 26 that applies only to the iPhone 12, addressing a compatibility issue with Australia's emergency calling system.

item.title: The Oscars to abandon broadcast TV, leaving longtime home of US network ABC
item.description: In a seismic shift for one of television’s marquee events, the Academy Awards will depart broadcast TV.

item.title: The TVs of tomorrow: Ultra-thin designs and 130-inch monsters
item.description: CES, the biggest technology show in the world, is happening this week in Las Vegas, a place for the biggest brands and the newest startups to demonstrate their innovation to the world in the hope of making it into retail stores this year.

item.title: 'One Battle After Another' dominates SAG's Actor Awards with seven nominations
item.description: One Battle After Another dominated nominations to the Actor Awards, with Paul Thomas Anderson's ragtag revolutionary saga landing a record seven nods in the annual SAG-AFTRA honours.

item.description: A﻿longside the tech giants at the CES trade show in Las Vegas are Australian start-ups displaying their products.

item.description: 2026 is going to be see some incredible video games released - from the highly anticipated Grand Theft Auto...

item.description: Here's what you need to know about Mario Tennis Fever, Pragmata and Resident Evil Requiem.

item.title: Seven years on, Fallout 76 developers still listening to fans and now watching the TV series
item.description: Seven years after its launch, video game Fallout 76 continues to evolve, with new updates shaped by both fan feedback and inspiration from Amazon's Fallout TV series.

item.title: Create your own Pokemon paradise with the latest in Nintendo life-sim
item.description: After an hour rebuilding my own deserted island back from the brink, it's safe to say our favourite pocket monsters remain irresistibly charming.

item.title: New God of War game headlines 'epic' PlayStation State of Play event
item.description: A new God of War game, exciting trailers, and several major reveals — PlayStation's February State of Play was one of the strongest showcases in recent memory.

item.description: A﻿lzheimer's is the biggest cause of dementia, which is the leading cause of death in Australia.

item.title: Samsung's new phone feature blocks prying eyes
item.description: Samsung's flagship smartphone for 2026 will launch with an industry-first privacy feature aimed at stopping those prying eyes around you from glimpsing what you're looking at on your screen.

item.title: Dire shortage of healthcare providers capable of performing potentially life-changing endometriosis scans
item.description: A life-changing ultrasound saved her from that fate, but there's a dire shortage of people capable of performing them in Australia.

item.title: Apple unveils $899 MacBook Neo in surprise launch
item.description: It's a brand new model for the tech giant, and it's a big shift away from the typical price point.

item.title: Australian markets suffer $90 billion wipeout as experts issue stark warning
item.description: At one stage today, the ASX had sunk by as much as 4.3 per cent, which would have put it on track for the steepest single-day drop since the COVID-19 pandemic in 2020.

item.description: A lot of people using older model phones are contemplating an upgrade.

item.description: Have you ever wanted to send a text about orcas or Bigfoot or trombones only to find you have to do it using words instead of pictures?

item.description: That's AUD, not USD. How does the laptop market survive in its current form when the MacBook Neo exists?

item.description: A﻿CMA found more than 370,000 emails the company sent were in breach of Australian law.

item.description: The CEO, one of Australia's richest men, said employees would get an email "within 20 minutes" to find out if they had lost their jobs.

item.description: T﻿he new king of brick-and-mortar retailers has been decided.

item.description: The owner said it was a "sad and difficult" decision to appoint administrators. ﻿

item.description: Radvinsky transformed the British-based company into an online pornography powerhouse.

item.description: Australians heading to the United States are set for a big change in the way we travel, with an all-new in-flight experience on the next-generation of aircraft from this airline.

item.description: The global fuel crisis has left one of the nation's most essential sectors heavily exposed.

item.title: Mining giant handed $2 billion in taxpayer funds to keep Australia's second-largest aluminium smelter running
item.description: Rio Tinto has secured a $2 billion taxpayer handout to keep Australia's second-largest aluminium smelter running in a bid to save 3000 jobs.

item.title: Sydney Opera House sails light up to mark 50 Years of Apple
item.description: The sails of the Sydney Opera House will light up with colour over the next three nights as part of a global celebration of Apple's 50th birthday.

item.description: Rebecca Foley, who co-owns Sprout Artisan Bakery in Brisbane, said ﻿unpredictable costs and new compliances have been creeping into her profit margin for years.

item.description: Mecca's parent company and associated businesses have each coughed up ﻿$198,000 in fines after being accused of late lodgements by the corporate watchdog.

item.description: The carpark construction, which would have created an additional 296 parking spots, would have come at the expense of the Hyper Karting track, axing more than 150 jobs in the process.

item.description: Janai Safar, 32, appeared in Sydney Downing Centre via video link today, where she was refused bail over charges relating to allegedly entering and remaining in a declared conflict zone and joining ISIS.

item.description: Partial results from local elections in England show big losses for Prime Minister Keir Starmer's governing Labour Party and gains for the hard-right party Reform UK.

item.description: The $20,000 threshold will apply per asset and will expand across tools, technology or other assets, both new and second-hand, and can be claimed in full at the end of every financial year.﻿

item.description: The 24-year-old left Christchurch, New Zealand, to become a member of a Catholic congregation focused on missionary work in Scotland. ﻿

item.title: Two boys arrested after alleged arson attack on Melbourne bar
item.description: I﻿t's the third time the popular venue has been attacked in the past month amid an apparent underworld war.

item.title: "This war, which should never have happened, has no need to continue."
item.description: A ship anchored off the United Arab Emirates was seized and taken toward Iran and another — a cargo ship ne...

item.title: Prominent Brisbane surgeon allowed to continue practising medicine despite rape charges
item.description: The medical regulator has elected not to take any immediate action against the bariatric surgeon.

item.title: Giving room instead of Tasing gran 'least of all evils'
item.description: Then-senior constable Kristian James Samuel White fired his weapon at the great-grandmother after being called to Yallambee Lodge nursing home at Cooma in southern NSW early on the morning of May 17, 2023.

item.title: Australian hantavirus evacuees start three-week quarantine
item.description: T﻿he group of six, which includes a New Zealand citizen, will quarantine in Perth for at least three weeks.

item.title: Ex-bikie's son kidnapped from home, found dead in park hours later
item.description: "It appears at this point in time that the [victim] was somewhat targeted."﻿

item.title: Leash tightens for double murderer and former fugitive
item.description: Damien Anthony Peters, now 57, killed and dismembered two of his lovers, Tereupii Akai and Bevan Frost, in 2001.

item.title: Trump, Xi wrap up summit claiming progress stabilising US-China relations
item.description: Presidents Donald Trump and Xi Jinping have wrapped up critical talks, claiming important progress in stabi...

item.title: Today in History - May 16: Outraged at an Australian court's light sentence, war criminal built his own prison
item.description: The war crimes trial of Japanese general Hitoshi Imamura by an Australian court ended on May 16, 1947.

item.title: Vinnies boss slams council decision to confiscate belongings from homeless camps as 'abhorrent'
item.description: The City of Port Phillip Council ﻿voted in favour of council officers confiscating and storing "encampment equipment" from people experiencing homelessness.

item.title: Home buyers tighten spending off the back of consecutive rate hikes
item.description: Back-to-back cash rate hikes have put a lid on some home buyer's budgets, according to analysis.

item.title: Trump's soft stance during China visit alarms Taiwan and its US supporters
item.description: Donald Trump has alarmed Taiwan and its supporters in Washington by appearing to side with China over its own independence.

item.title: $134,000 statue for Dan Andrews slammed as tone deaf by businesses and politicians
item.description: Businesses and politicians have slammed a plan to erect a statue of former Victorian Premier Daniel Andrews, calling it a waste of taxpayer money.

item.title: Police warn international students about Chinese 'authority scams' after reported losses of more than $400,000
item.description: Some report they have lost more than $400,000.

item.title: Young tourist killed in horror Queensland bus crash identified as Argentine-Italian national
item.description: The 26-year-old was on a FlixBus partner coach travelling from Cairns to ﻿Airlie Beach before it rolled off the road.

item.title: Exclusive Brethren sect denies telling members to kill their pets
item.description: A prominent sect has denied telling members to euthanise their pets, but confirmed they have been urged to get rid of them.

item.title: Confused driverless taxis take over streets in American suburb
item.description: Dozens of driverless Waymo vehicles have been seen circling through an Atlanta neighbourhood without riders.

item.title: Judge declares a mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s rape retrial after jury deadlocks
item.description: While the former Hollywood mogul has been convicted of other sex crimes on two US coasts and remains behind bars, the mistrial leaves the New York rape charge in limbo.

item.title: Man set to front court after alleged hit-and-run in Sydney's Inner West
item.description: A 67-year-old woman underwent surgery for lower-body injuries after being pinned against the wall of the cafe.

item.title: Police probe death of couple found in Melbourne home
item.description: A﻿uthorities believe the pair were known to each other.Mystery surrounds the deaths of a man and woman after their bodies were found in Melbourne home.

item.title: Trump says Islamic State group leader was killed in a joint US-Nigerian mission
item.description: Trump announced the joint operation in Africa’s most populous country in a late-night social media post that offered few details.

item.title: Boy killed, two other teenagers fighting for life after ute hits tree
item.description: A﻿ 14-year-old boy has been killed and two other teenagers are fighting for life after a ute believed to have been stolen hit a tree in rural Victoria.

item.title: Negative gearing change put to test in Saturday auctions
item.description: Hopes were a little higher this morning among those looking to break into the property market after the federal budget.

item.title: Two teenage girls charged after Woolworths store sent into lockdown
item.description: Two teenagers have been charged after allegedly sending a Brisbane Woolworths store into lockdown.

item.title: Man charged after allegedly taking axe to Sydney supermarket
item.description: A man has been charged after he allegedly threatened an innocent Woolworths supermarket worker with an axe in Sydney's north-west.

item.title: Neo-Nazi group 'White Australia' listed as banned hate group
item.description: W﻿hite Australia, the neo-Nazi group, has been listed as a banned hate group under the government's new laws.

item.title: Fears for welfare of man, 96, missing for over a week
item.description: A 96-year-old man has gone missing in Melbourne.

item.title: Claim Queensland bus hit guardrail a day before fatal crash
item.description: The coach involved in a deadly bus crash in Queensland allegedly struck a guardrail a day earlier.

item.title: 'When's it our turn?': Mum who lost son to deadly infection says NSW is now the outlier on vaccines
item.description: New South Wales mum Stacey Chater lost her son Brayden to a meningococcal B infection, but says for years her pleas have fallen on deaf ears.

item.title: Oscar, 16, is calling on the NSW government to trial 50c public transport fares
item.description: T﻿he 16-year-old is calling on the NSW government to trial 50c fares as the cost of living crisis bites.

item.title: Today in History - May 17: Russian TV audience tricked into believing Lenin was a mushroom
item.description: Russian television viewers were stunned, baffled, bemused or alarmed by a live news report making a bizarre accusation about an iconic former leader on May 17, 1991.

item.title: Maldives suspends search for four bodies in underwater cave after military diver dies
item.description: A Maldivian military diver has died while searching for the bodies of four Italian divers believed to be deep inside an underwater cave.

item.title: Trump's Beijing visit was more vibes than details. And Xi set the tone
item.description: C﻿hina's leader also sent an ominous message if a key "red line" was crossed.

item.title: Four people fighting for life after driver ploughs into pedestrians in Modena, Italy
item.description: O﻿ne woman reportedly had to have both legs amputated after being pinned against a shop window.

item.title: Beaches remain open on Rottnest Island after man killed in 'horrific' shark attack
item.description: The 38-year-old man was attacked by a four-metre-long great white shark.

item.title: Suspects on the run as Gold Coast bus crashes after being pelted with rocks
item.description: T﻿he bus driver was taken to hospital after the surprise attack.

item.title: Dozens arrested as rival protests and FA Cup final descend on London
item.description: Police flooded the city with 4000 officers and high-tech gear to head off any potential chaos.

item.title: Passenger banned from Qantas flights after plane diverted amid claims crew member was 'bitten'
item.description: T﻿he plane was forced to stop in Tahiti after a passenger became belligerent to crew and passengers.

item.title: Perth spearfisher killed by shark remembered as devoted father and ocean lover
item.description: Steven Mattaboni is survived by his loving wife and two young daughters, including a four-month-old.

item.title: Neighbours wake to screams before man dies in suspicious balcony fall at unit block
item.description: Neighbours have described waking to screams after a man died in a suspected balcony fall at a unit complex in Castle Hill overnight.

item.title: Residents jolted awake as police pursuit ends in wrecked street
item.description: Residents were jolted awake after an alleged high-speed chase ended with a car smashing into three parked vehicles.

item.title: Australians quarantining in Perth learn their hantavirus fate
item.description: The four Australian citizens, one permanent resident and a New Zealand national quarantining for three weeks in Western Australia's Bullsbrook Centre for National Resilience received their blood test results today.

item.title: New post-budget poll reveals mixed verdict for Albanese government
item.description: For the first time, Angus Taylor has edged ahead of Anthony Albanese as preferred prime minister.

item.title: Drone strike sparks a fire on the perimeter of UAE's nuclear power plant, shaking Iran war ceasefire
item.description: T﻿he shaky ceasefire is under further strain as suspicion falls on Iran.

item.title: WHO declares global health emergency over Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda
item.description: The current outbreak is caused by a rare variant that has no approved therapeutics or vaccines.

item.title: Ukrainian drone strikes on Russia kill four and wound 12 others, while debris lands on Moscow airport
item.description: At least four people are dead after ne of Ukraine's largest drone strikes on Russia.
